45→import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n    46→\n    47→/**\n    48→ * An active task could only be in one of the following status:\n    49→ *\n    50→ * 1. It's assigned to one of the executors for processing.\n    51→ * 2. It's locked for committing, removal, other manipulations etc.\n    52→ * 3. Neither 1 or 2, i.e. it stays idle. This is possible if we do not have enough executors or because those tasks\n    53→ *    are not processable (e.g. because no records fetched) yet.\n    54→ */\n    55→public final class DefaultTaskManager implements TaskManager {\n    56→\n    57→    private final Time time;\n    58→    private final Logger log;\n    59→    private final TasksRegistry tasks;\n    60→    private final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata;\n    61→\n    62→    private final Lock tasksLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    63→    private final Condition tasksCondition = tasksLock.newCondition();\n    64→    private final List<TaskId> lockedTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n    65→    private final Map<TaskId, StreamsException> uncaughtExceptions = new HashMap<>();\n    66→    private final Map<TaskId, TaskExecutor> assignedTasks = new HashMap<>();\n    67→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    68→\n    69→    private final List<TaskExecutor> taskExecutors;\n    70→\n    71→    public static class DefaultTaskExecutorCreator implements TaskExecutorCreator {\n    72→        @Override\n    73→        public TaskExecutor create(final TaskManager taskManager, final String name, final Time time, final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n    74→            return new DefaultTaskExecutor(taskManager, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata);\n    75→        }\n    76→    }\n    77→\n    78→    public DefaultTaskManager(final Time time,\n    79→                              final String clientId,\n    80→                              final TasksRegistry tasks,\n    81→                              final TaskExecutorCreator executorCreator,\n    82→                              final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata,\n    83→                              final int numExecutors,\n    84→                              final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata\n    85→                              ) {\n    86→        final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", clientId);\n    87→        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n    88→        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskManager.class);\n    89→        this.time = time;\n    90→        this.tasks = tasks;\n    91→        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n    92→        this.topologyMetadata = topologyMetadata;\n    93→        topologyMetadata.registerLifecycleObserver(new TaskTopologyObserver());\n    94→\n    95→        this.taskExecutors = new ArrayList<>(numExecutors);\n    96→        for (int i = 1; i <= numExecutors; i++) {\n    97→            final String name = clientId + \"-TaskExecutor-\" + i;\n    98→            this.taskExecutors.add(executorCreator.create(this, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata));\n    99→        }\n   100→    }\n   101→\n   102→    private class TaskTopologyObserver implements TopologyMetadata.TopologyLifecycleObserver {\n   103→        @Override\n   104→        public void onTopologyChange(final long newVersion, final Set<String> activeTopologies) {\n   105→            validateTopologyConsistency(activeTopologies);\n   106→        }\n   107→    }\n   108→\n   109→    private void validateTopologyConsistency(final Set<String> activeTopologies) {\n   110→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   111→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   112→                final String topologyName = task.id().topologyName();\n   113→                if (topologyName != null && !activeTopologies.contains(topologyName)) {\n   114→                    log.warn(\"Task {} belongs to topology {} which is no longer active\", task.id(), topologyName);\n   115→                }\n   116→            }\n   117→        });\n   118→    }\n   119→\n   120→    @Override\n   121→    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n   122→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   123→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   124→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for getting next task to assign is unrecognized\");\n   125→            }\n   126→\n   127→            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n   128→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   129→                if (topologyMetadata.isTaskTopologyStale(task.id())) {\n   130→                    continue;\n   131→                }\n   132→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   133→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   134→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   135→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   136→                ) {\n   137→\n   138→                    assignedTasks.put(task.id(), executor);\n   139→\n   140→                    log.debug(\"Assigned task {} to executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   141→\n   142→                    return task;\n   143→                }\n   144→  @Override\n   153→    public void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n   154→        final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   155→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   156→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   157→ 161→                    log.debug(\"Await unblocked: returning early from await since a processable task {} was found\", task.id());\n   162→                    return false;\n   163→                }\n   164→            }\n   165→            try {\n   166→                // We re-check the shutdownRequest atomic boolean to avoid a race condition. If this thread was\n   167→                // previously interrupted while awaiting tasksCondition, it is possible to miss the signalAll that\n   168→                // is called during shutdown. If this happens, we end up blocking in the await forever.\n   169→                if (!isShuttingDown.get()) {\n   170→                    log.debug(\"Await blocking\");\n   171→ (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n   176→                // we interrupt the thread for shut down and pause.\n   177→                // we can ignore this exception.\n   178→ allInputTopicsCopy.addAll(newInputTopics);\n   377→\n   378→        if (allInputTopicsCopy.size() != numInputTopics + numNewInputTopics) {\n   379→ Topologies\");\n   390→            } else if (this.globalTopology != null) {\n   391→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology builder had global state, but global topology has already 404→        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n   405→            if (hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n   406→                log.error(\"Detected a named topology with no input topics, a named topology may not be STATE_UPDATER_ID_SUBSTRING = \"-StateUpdater-\";\n   114→\n   115→    /**\n   116→     * Stream thread states are the possible states that a stream thread can be in.\n   117→     * A thread must only be in one state at a time\n   118→     * The expected state transitions with the following defined states is:\n   119→     *\n   120→     * <pre>\n   121→     *                 +-------------+\n   122→     *          +<---- | Created (0) |\n   123→     *          |      +-----+-------+\n   124→     *          |            |\n   125→     *          |            v\n   126→     *          |      +-----+-------+\n   127→     *          +<---- | Starting (1)|----->+\n   128→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   129→     *          |                           |\n   130→     *          |            +<----------+  |\n   131→     *          |            |           |  |\n   132→     *          |            v           |  |\n   133→     *          |      +-----+-------+   |  |\n   134→     *          +<---- | Partitions  | --+  |\n   135→     *          |      | Revoked (2) | <----+\n   136→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   137→     *          |           |  ^            |\n   138→     *          |           v  |            |\n   139→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   140→     *          +<---- | Partitions  |      |\n   141→     *          |      | Assigned (3)| <----+\n   142→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   143→     *          |            |              |\n   144→     *          |            +<----------+  |\n   145→     *          |            |           |  |\n   146→     *          |            v           |  |\n   147→     *          |      +-----+-------+   |  |\n   148→     *          |      |             | --+     |      +-----+-------+\n   154→     *          +----> | Pending     |\n   155→     *                 | Shutdown (5)|\n   156→     *                 +-----+-------+\n   157→     *                       |\n   158→     *                       v\n   159→     *                 +-----+-------+\n   160→     *                 | Dead (6)    |\n   161→     *                 +-------------+\n   162→     * </pre>\n   163→     *\n   164→     * Note the following:\n   165→     * <ul>\n   166→     *     <li>Any state can go to PENDING_SHUTDOWN. That is because streams can be closed at any time.</li>\n   167→     *     <li>\n   168→     *         State PENDING_SHUTDOWN may want to transit to some other states other than DEAD,\n   169→     *         in the corner case when the shutdown is triggered while the thread is still in the rebalance loop.\n   170→     *         In this case we will forbid the transition but will not treat as an error.\n   171→     *     </li>\n   172→     *     <li>\n   173→     *         State PARTITIONS_REVOKED may want transit to itself indefinitely, in the corner case when\n   174→     *         the coordinator repeatedly fails in-between revoking partitions and assigning new partitions.\n   175→     *         Also during streams instance start up PARTITIONS_REVOKED may want to transit to itself as well.\n   176→     *         In this case we will allow the transition but it will be a no-op as the set of revoked partitions\n   177→     *         should be empty.\n   178→     *     </li>\n   179→     * </ul>\n   180→     */\n   181→    public enum State implements ThreadStateTransitionValidator {\n   182→\n   183→        CREATED(1, 5),                    // 0\n   184→        STARTING(2, 3, 5),                // 1\n   185→        PARTITIONS_REVOKED(2, 3, 5),      // 2\n   186→        PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED(2, 3, 4, 5),  // 3\n   187→        RUNNING(2, 3, 4, 5),              // 4\n   188→        PENDING_SHUTDOWN(6),              // 5\n   189→        DEAD;                             // 6\n   190→\n   191→        private final Set<Integer> validTransitions = new HashSet<>();\n   192→\n   193→        State(final Integer... validTransitions) {\n   194→            this.validTransitions.addAll(Arrays.asList(validTransitions));\n   195→        }\n   196→\n   197→        public boolean isAlive() {\n   198→            return equals(RUNNING) || equals(STARTING) || equals(PARTITIONS_REVOKED) || equals(PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED);\n   199→        }\n   200→\n   201→        @Override\n   202→        public boolean isValidTransition(final ThreadStateTransitionValidator newState) {\n   203→            final State tmpState = (State) newState;\n   204→            return validTransitions.contains(tmpState.ordinal());\n   205→        }\n   206→    }\n   207→\n   208→    /**\n   209→     * Listen to state change events\n   210→     */\n   211→    public interface StateListener {\n   212→\n   213→        /**\n   214→         * Called when state changes\n   215→         *\n   216→         * @param thread   thread changing state\n   217→         * @param newState current state\n   218→         * @param oldState previous state\n   219→         */\n   220→        void onChange(final Thread thread, final ThreadStateTransitionValidator newState, final ThreadStateTransitionValidator oldState);\n   221→    }\n   222→\n   223→    /**\n   224→     * Set the {@link StreamThread.StateListener} to be notified when state changes. Note this API is internal to\n   225→     * Kafka Streams and is not intended to be used by an external application.\n   226→     */\n   227→    public void setStateListener(final StreamThread.StateListener listener) {\n   228→        stateListener = listener;\n   229→    }\n   230→\n   231→    public StreamThread.StateListener getStateListener() {\n   232→        return stateListener;\n   233→    }\n   234→\n   235→    /**\n   236→     * @return The state this instance is in\n   237→     */\n   238→    public State state() {\n   239→        // we do not need to use the state lock since the variable is volatile\n   240→        return state;\n   241→    }\n   242→\n   243→    void setPartitionAssignedTime(final long lastPartitionAssignedMs) {\n   244→        this.lastPartitionAssignedMs = lastPartitionAssignedMs;\n   245→    }\n   246→\n   247→    /**\n   248→     * Sets the state\n   249→     *\n   250→     * @param newState New state\n   251→     * @return The state prior to the call to setState, or null if the transition is invalid\n   252→     */\n   253→    State setState(final State newState) {\n   254→        final State oldState;\n   255→\n   256→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   257→            oldState = state;\n   258→\n   259→            if (state == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN && newState != State.DEAD) {\n   260→                log.debug(\"Ignoring request to transit from PENDING_SHUTDOWN to {}: \" +\n   261→                              \"only DEAD state is a valid next state\", newState);\n   262→                // when the state is already in PENDING_SHUTDOWN, all other transitions will be\n   263→                // refused but we do not throw exception here\n   264→                return null;\n   265→            } else if (state == State.DEAD) {\n   266→                log.debug(\"Ignoring request to transit from DEAD to {}: \" +\n   267→                              \"no valid next state after DEAD\", newState);\n   268→                // when the state is already in NOT_RUNNING, all its transitions\n   269→                // will be refused but we do not throw exception here\n   270→                return null;\n   271→            } else if (!state.isValidTransition(newState)) {\n   272→                log.error(\"Unexpected state transition from {} to {}\", oldState, newState);\n   273→                throw new StreamsException(logPrefix + \"Unexpected state transition from \" + oldState + \" to \" + newState);\n   274→            } else {\n   275→                log.info(\"State transition from {} to {}\", oldState, newState);\n   276→            }\n   277→\n   278→            state = newState;\n   279→            if (newState == State.RUNNING) {\n   280→                updateThreadMetadata(taskManager.activeTaskMap(), taskManager.standbyTaskMap());\n   281→            }\n   282→\n   283→            stateLock.notifyAll();\n   284→        }\n   285→\n   286→        if (stateListener != null) {\n   287→            stateListener.onChange(this, state, oldState);\n   288→        }\n   289→\n   290→        return oldState;\n   291→    }\n   292→\n   293→    public boolean isRunning() {\n   294→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   295→            return state.isAlive();\n   296→        }\n   297→    }\n   298→\n   299→    public boolean PositionBound positionBound,\n   179→                                           final QueryConfig config) {\n   180→        if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE_WITH_HEADERS) {\n   181→            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Queries (IQv2) are not supported for timestamped key-value stores with headers yet.\");\n   182→        }\n   183→        QueryResult<R>            }\n   205→\n   206→            // We don't need to check the position at the state store since we already performed the check on\n   207→            // the merged position above\n   208→ 217→                                    final InternalProcessorContext<?, ?> context) {\n   218→        if (flushListener != null) {\n   219→            final byte[] rawNewValue = entry.newValue();\n   220→            final byte[] rawOldValue = rawNewValue == null || sendOldValues ? wrapped().get(entry.key()) : null;\n   221→\n   222→            // this is an optimization: if this key did not exist in underlying store and also not in the cache,\n   223→            // we can skip flushing to downstream as well as writing to underlying store\n   224→            if (rawNewValue != null || context.recordContext();\n   227→                try {\n   228→                    context.setRecordContext(entry.entry().context());\n   229→                    wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.entry().context().headers()));\n   236→                } finally {\n   237→                    context.setRecordContext(current);\n   238→                }\n   239→            }\n   240→ 244→                wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.newValue());\n   245→            } finally {\n   246→                context.setRecordContext(current);\n   247→            }\n   248→        }\n    private static final String BUG_ERROR_MESSAGE = \"This indicates a bug. \" +\n    80→        \"Please report at https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/KAFKA/issues or to the dev-mailing list (https://kafka.apache.org/contact).\";\n    81→\n    82→    private class StateUpdaterThread extends Thread {\n    83→\n    84→        private final ChangelogReader changelogReader;\n    85→        private final StateUpdaterMetrics updaterMetrics;\n    86→        private final AtomicBoolean isRunning = new AtomicBoolean(true);\n    87→        private final AtomicBoolean isIdle = new AtomicBoolean(false);\n    88→        private final Map<TaskId, Task> updatingTasks = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n    89→        private final Map<TaskId, Task> pausedTasks = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n    90→\n    91→        private final WindowedSum idleTimeWindowedSum = new WindowedSum();\n    92→        private final WindowedSum checkpointTimeWindowedSum = new WindowedSum();\n    93→        private final WindowedSum activeRestoreTimeWindowedSum = new WindowedSum();\n    94→        private final WindowedSum standbyRestoreTimeWindowedSum = new      private volatile KafkaFutureImpl<Uuid> clientInstanceIdFuture = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n   104→\n   105→        public StateUpdaterThread(final String name,\n   106→                                  final StreamsMetricsImpl metrics,\n   107→                                  final ChangelogReader changelogReader) {\n   108→            super(name);\n   109→            this.changelogReader = changelogReader;\n   110→            this.updaterMetrics = new StateUpdaterMetrics(metrics, name);\n   111→            this.metricsConfig = metrics.metricsRegistry().config();\n   112→        }\n   113→\n   114→        public Collection<Task> updatingTasks() {\n   115→            return updatingTasks.values();\n   116→        }\n   117→\n   118→        public Collection<StandbyTask> updatingStandbyTasks() {\n   119→            return updatingTasks.values().stream()\n   120→                .filter(t -> !t.isActive())\n   121→                .map(t -> (StandbyTask) t)\n   122→                .collect(Collectors.toList());\n   123→        }\n   124→\n   125→        private boolean onlyStandbyTasksUpdating() {\n   126→            return !updatingTasks.isEmpty() && updatingTasks.values().stream().noneMatch(Task::isActive);\n   127→        }\n   128→\n   129→        public Collection<Task> pausedTasks() {\n   130→            return pausedTasks.values();\n   131→        }\n   132→\n   133→        public long numUpdatingStandbyTasks() {\n   134→            return updatingTasks.values().stream()\n   135→                .filter(t -> !t.isActive())\n   136→                .count();\n   137→        }\n   138→\n   139→        public long numRestoringActiveTasks() {\n   140→            return updatingTasks.values().stream()\n   141→                .filter(Task::isActive)\n   142→                .count();\n   143→        }\n   144→\n   145→        public long numPausedStandbyTasks() {\n   146→            return pausedTasks.values().stream()\n   147→                .filter(t -> !t.isActive())\n   148→                .count();\n   149→        }\n   150→\n   151→        public long numPausedActiveTasks() {\n   152→            return pausedTasks.values().stream()\n   153→                .filter(Task::isActive)\n   154→                .count();\n   155→        }\n   156→\n   157→        @Override\n   158→        public void run() {\n   159→            log.info(\"State updater thread started\");\n   160→            try {\n   161→                initTimeWindowIfNeeded(time.milliseconds());\n   162→                while (isRunning.get()) {\n   163→                    runOnce();\n   164→                }\n   165→            } catch (final RuntimeException anyOtherException) {\n   166→                handleRuntimeException(anyOtherException);\n   167→            } finally {\n   168→                clearInputQueue();\n   169→                clearUpdatingAndPausedTasks();\n   170→                updaterMetrics.clear();\n   171→                log.info(\"State updater thread stopped\");\n   172→            }\n   173→        }\n   174→\n   175→        private void clearInputQueue() {\n   176→            tasksAndActionsLock.lock();\n   177→            try {\n   178→                tasksAndActions.clear();\n   179→            } finally {\n   180→                tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n   181→            }\n   182→        }\n   183→\n   184→        // In each iteration:\n   185→        //   1) check if updating tasks need to be paused\n   186→        //   2) check if paused tasks need to be resumed\n   187→        //   3) restore those updating tasks\n   188→        //   4) checkpoint those updating task states\n   189→        //   5) idle waiting if there is no more tasks to be restored\n   190→        //\n   191→        //   Note that, 1-3) are measured as restoring time, while 4) and 5) measured separately\n   192→        //   as checkpointing time and idle time\n   193→        private void runOnce() {\n   194→            final long totalStartTimeMs = time.milliseconds();\n   195→            performActionsOnTasks();\n   196→\n   197→            resumeTasks();\n   198→            pauseTasks();\n   199→            restoreTasks(totalStartTimeMs);\n   200→\n   201→            maybeGetClientInstanceIds();\n   202→\n   203→            final long checkpointStartTimeMs = time.milliseconds();\n   204→            maybeCheckpointTasks(checkpointStartTimeMs);\n   205→\n   206→            final long waitStartTimeMs = time.milliseconds();\n   207→            waitIfAllChangelogsCompletelyRead();\n   208→\n   209→            final long endTimeMs = time.milliseconds();\n   210→            final long totalWaitTime = Math.max(0L, endTimeMs - waitStartTimeMs);\n   211→            final long totalTime = Math.max(0L, endTimeMs - totalStartTimeMs);\n   212→\n   213→            recordMetrics(endTimeMs, totalTime, totalWaitTime);\n   214→        }\n   215→\n   216→        private void performActionsOnTasks() {\n   217→            tasksAndActionsLock.lock();\n   218→            try {\n   219→                for (final TaskAndAction taskAndAction : tasksAndActions()) {\n   220→                    final Action action = taskAndAction.action();\n   221→                    switch (action) {\n   222→                        case ADD:\n   223→                            addTask(taskAndAction.task());\n   224→                            break;\n   225→                        case REMOVE:\n   226→                            removeTask(taskAndAction.taskId(), taskAndAction.futureForRemove(), taskAndAction.suspendReason());\n   227→                            break;\n   228→                        default:\n   229→                            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Unknown action type \" + action);\n   230→                    }\n   231→                }\n   232→            } finally {\n   233→                tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n   234→            }\n   235→        }\n   236→\n   237→        private void resumeTasks() {\n   238→            if (isTopologyResumed.compareAndSet(true, false)) {\n   239→                for (final Task task : pausedTasks.values()) {\n   240→                    if (!topologyMetadata.isPaused(task.id().topologyName())) {\n   241→                        resumeTask(task);\n   242→                    }\n   243→                }\n   244→            }\n   245→        }\n   246→\n   247→        private void pauseTasks() {\n   248→            for (final Task task : updatingTasks.values()) {\n   249→                if (topologyMetadata.isPaused(task.id().topologyName())) {\n   250→                    pauseTask(task);\n   251→                }\n   252→            }\n   253→        }\n   254→\n   255→        private void restoreTasks(final long now) {\n   256→            try {\n   257→                final long restored = changelogReader.restore(updatingTasks);\n   258→                updaterMetrics.restoreSensor.record(restored, now);\n   259→            } catch (final TaskCorruptedException taskCorruptedException) {\n   260→                handleTaskCorruptedException(taskCorruptedException);\n   261→            } catch (final StreamsException streamsException) {\n   262→                handleStreamsException(streamsException);\n   263→            }\n   264→            final Set<TopicPartition> completedChangelogs = changelogReader.completedChangelogs();\n   265→            final    /**\n    79→     * The states that the global stream thread can be in\n    80→     *\n    81→     * <pre>\n    82→     *                +-------------+\n    83→     *          +<--- | Created (0) |\n    84→     *          |     +-----+-------+\n    85→     *          |           |\n    86→     *          |           v\n    87→     *          |     +-----+-------+\n    88→     *          +<---  98→     *                +-----+-------+\n    99→     *                | Dead (3)    |\n   100→     *                +-------------+\n   101→     * </pre>\n   102→     *\n   103→     * Note the following:\n   104→     * <ul>\n   105→     *     <li>Any state can go to PENDING_SHUTDOWN. That is because streams can be closed at any time.</li>\n   106→     *     <li>State PENDING_SHUTDOWN may want to transit itself. In this case we will forbid the transition but will not treat as an error.</li>\n   107→     * </ul>\n   108→     */\n   109→    public enum State implements ThreadStateTransitionValidator {\n   110→        CREATED(1, 2), RUNNING(2), PENDING_SHUTDOWN(3), DEAD;\n   111→\n   112→        private final Set<Integer> validTransitions = new HashSet<>();\n   113→\n   114→        State(final Integer... validTransitions) {\n   115→            this.validTransitions.addAll(Arrays.asList(validTransitions));\n   116→        }\n   117→\n   118→        public boolean isRunning() {\n   119→            return equals(RUNNING);\n   120→        }\n   121→\n   122→        public boolean inErrorState() {\n   123→            return equals(DEAD) || equals(PENDING_SHUTDOWN);\n   124→        }\n   125→\n   126→        @Override\n   127→        public boolean isValidTransition(final ThreadStateTransitionValidator newState) {\n   128→            final State tmpState = (State) newState;\n   129→            return validTransitions.contains(tmpState.ordinal());\n   130→        }\n   131→    }\n   132→\n   133→    private volatile State state = State.CREATED;\n   134→    private final Object stateLock = new Object();\n   135→    private StreamThread.StateListener stateListener = null;\n   136→    private final String logPrefix;\n   137→    private final StateRestoreListener stateRestoreListener;\n   138→\n   139→    /**\n   140→     * Set the {@link StreamThread.StateListener} to be notified when state changes. Note this API is internal to\n   141→     * Kafka Streams and is not intended to be used by an external application.\n   142→     */\n   143→    public void setStateListener(final StreamThread.StateListener listener) {\n   144→        stateListener = listener;\n   145→    }\n   146→\n   147→    /**\n   148→     * @return The state this instance is in\n   149→     */\n   150→    public State state() {\n   151→        // we do not need to use the stat lock since the variable is volatile\n   152→        return state;\n   153→    }\n   154→\n   155→    /**\n   156→     * Sets the state\n   157→     *\n   158→     * @param newState New state\n   159→     */\n   160→    private void setState(final State newState) {\n   161→        final State oldState = state;\n   162→\n   163→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   164→            if (state == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN && newState == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN) {\n   165→                // when the state is already in PENDING_SHUTDOWN, its transition to itself\n   166→                // will be refused but we do not throw exception here\n   167→                return;\n   168→            } else if (state == State.DEAD) {\n   169→                // when the state is already in NOT_RUNNING, all its transitions\n   170→                // will be refused but we do not throw exception here\n   171→                return;\n   172→            } else if (!state.isValidTransition(newState)) {\n   173→                log.error(\"Unexpected state transition from {} to {}\", oldState, newState);\n   174→                throw new StreamsException(logPrefix + \"Unexpected state transition from \" + oldState + \" to \" + newState);\n   175→            } else {\n   176→                log.info(\"State transition from {} to {}\", oldState, newState);\n   177→            }\n   178→\n   179→            state = newState;\n   180→        }\n   181→\n   182→        if (stateListener != null) {\n   183→            stateListener.onChange(this, state, oldState);\n   184→        }\n   185→    }\n   186→\n   187→    public boolean stillRunning() {\n   188→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   189→            return state.isRunning();\n   190→        }\n   191→    }\n   192→\n   193→    public boolean inErrorState() {\n   194→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   195→            return state.inErrorState();\n   196→        }\n   197→    }\n   198→\n   199→    public GlobalStreamThread(final ProcessorTopology topology,\n   200→                              final StreamsConfig config,\n   201→                              final Consumer<byte[], byte[]> globalConsumer,\n   202→                              final StateDirectory stateDirectory,\n   203→                              final long cacheSizeBytes,\n   204→                              final StreamsMetricsImpl streamsMetrics,\n   205→                              final Time time,\n   206→                              final String threadClientId,\n   207→                              final StateRestoreListener stateRestoreListener,\n   208→                              final java.util.function.Consumer<Throwable> streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler) {\n   209→        super(threadClientId);\n   210→        this.time = time;\n   211→        this.config = config;\n   212→        this.topology = topology;\n   213→        this.globalConsumer = globalConsumer;\n   214→        this.stateDirectory = stateDirectory;\n   215→        this.streamsMetrics = streamsMetrics;\n   216→        this.logPrefix = String.format(\"global-stream-thread [%s] \", threadClientId);\n   217→        this.logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n   218→        this.log = logContext.logger(getClass());\n   219→        this.cache = new ThreadCache(logContext, cacheSizeBytes, this.streamsMetrics);\n   220→        this.stateRestoreListener = stateRestoreListener;\n   221→        this.streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler = streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler;\n   222→        this.cacheSize = new AtomicLong(-1L);\n   223→    }\n   224→\n   225→    static class StateConsumer {\n   226→        private final Consumer<byte[], byte[]> globalConsumer;\n   227→        private final GlobalStateMaintainer stateMaintainer;\n   228→        private final Duration pollTime;\n   229→        private final Logger log;\n   230→\n   231→        StateConsumer(final LogContext logContext,\n   232→                      final Consumer<byte[], byte[]> globalConsumer,\n   233→                      final GlobalStateMaintainer stateMaintainer,\n   234→                      final Duration pollTime) {\n   235→            this.log = logContext.logger(getClass());\n   236→            this.globalConsumer = globalConsumer;\n   237→            this.stateMaintainer = stateMaintainer;\n   238→            this.pollTime = pollTime;\n   239→        }\n   240→\n   241→        /**\n   242→         * @throws IllegalStateException If a store gets registered after initialized is already finished\n   243→         * @throws StreamsException      if the store's change log does not contain the partition\n   244→         */\n   245→        void initialize() {\n   246→            final Map<TopicPartition, Long> partitionOffsets = stateMaintainer.initialize();\n   247→            globalConsumer.assign(partitionOffsets.keySet());\n   248→            for (final Map.Entry<TopicPartition, Long> entry : partitionOffsets.entrySet()) {\n   249→                globalConsumer.seek(entry.getKey(), entry.getValue());\n   250→            }\n   251→        }\n   252→\n   253→        void pollAndUpdate() {\n   254→            final ConsumerRecords<byte[], byte[]> received = globalConsumer.poll(pollTime);\n   255→            for (final ConsumerRecord<byte[], byte[]> record : received) {\n   256→                stateMaintainer.update(record);\n   257→            }\n   258→            stateMaintainer.maybeCheckpoint();\n   259→        }\n   260→\n   261→        public void close(final boolean wipeStateStore) throws IOException {\n   262→            try {\n   263→                globalConsumer.close();\n   264→            } catch (final RuntimeException e) {\n   265→                // just log an error if the consumer throws an exception during close\n   266→                // so we can always attempt to close the state stores.\n   267→                log.error(\"Failed to close global consumer due to the following error:\", e);\n   268→            }\n   269→\n   270→            stateMaintainer.close(wipeStateStore);\n   271→        }\n   272→    }\n   273→\n   274→    @Override\n   275→    public void run() {\n   276→        final StateConsumer stateConsumer = initialize();\n   277→\n   278→        if (stateConsumer == null) {\n   279→            // during initialization, the caller thread would wait for the state consumer\n   280→            // to restore the global state store before transiting to RUNNING state and return;\n   281→            // if an error happens during the restoration process, the stateConsumer will be null\n   282→            // and in this case we will transit the state to PENDING_SHUTDOWN and DEAD immediately.\n   283→            // the exception will be thrown in the caller thread during start() function.\n   284→            setState(State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN);\n   285→            setState(State.DEAD);\n   286→\n   287→            log.error(\"Error happened during initialization of the global state store; this thread has shutdown.\");\n   288→            streamsMetrics.removeAllThreadLevelSensors(getName());\n   289→            streamsMetrics.removeAllThreadLevelMetrics(getName());\n   290→\n   291→            return;\n   292→        }\n   293→\n   294→        boolean wipeStateStore = false;\n   295→        try {\n   296→            while (stillRunning()) {\n   297→                final long size = cacheSize.getAndSet(-1L);\n   298→                if (size != -1L) {\n   299→                    cache.resize(size);\n   300→                }\n   301→                stateConsumer.pollAndUpdate();\n   302→\n   303→                if (fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId != -1) {\n   304→                    if (fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId >= time.milliseconds()) {\n   305→                        try {\n   306→                            // we pass in a timeout of zero, to just trigger the \"get instance id\" background RPC,\n   307→                            // we don't want to block the global thread that can do useful work in the meantime\n   308→                            clientInstanceIdFuture.complete(globalConsumer.clientInstanceId(Duration.ZERO));\n   309→                            fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId = -1;\n   310→                        } catch (final IllegalStateException disabledError) {\n   311→                            // if telemetry is disabled on a client, we swallow the error,\n   312→                            // to allow returning a partial result for all other clients\n   313→                            clientInstanceIdFuture.complete(null);\n   314→                            fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId = -1;\n   315→                        } catch (final TimeoutException swallow) {\n   316→                            // swallow\n   317→                        } catch (final Exception error) {\n   318→                            clientInstanceIdFuture.completeExceptionally(error);\n   319→                            fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId = -1;\n   320→                        }\n   321→                    } else {\n   322→                        clientInstanceIdFuture.completeExceptionally(\n   323→                            new TimeoutException(\"Could not retrieve global consumer client instance id.\")\n   324→                        );\n   325→                        fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId = -1;\n   326→                    }\n   327→                }\n   328→            }\n   329→        } catch (final InvalidOffsetException recoverableException) {\n   330→            wipeStateStore = true;\n   331→            log.error(\n   332→                \"Updating global state failed due to inconsistent local state. Will attempt to clean up the local state. You can restart KafkaStreams to recover from this error.\",\n   333→                recoverableException\n   334→            );\n   335→            final StreamsException e = new StreamsException(\n   336→                \"Updating global state failed. You can restart KafkaStreams to launch a new GlobalStreamThread to recover from this error.\",\n   337→                recoverableException\n   338→            );\n   339→            this.streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler.accept(e);\n   340→        } catch (final Exception e) {\n   341→            log.error(\"Error happened while maintaining global state store. The streams application or client will now close to ERROR.\", e);\n   342→            this.streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler.accept(e);\n   343→        } finally {\n   344→            // set the state to pending shutdown first as it may be called due to error;\n   345→            // its state may already be PENDING_SHUTDOWN so it will return false but we\n   346→            // intentionally do not check the returned flag\n   347→            setState(State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN);\n   348→\n   349→            log.info(\"Shutting down\");\n   350→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01LubCALdDREF8pSK2d9HW2j","session_id":"385b3ce2-4bf0-4c25-8575-4b3d9846043b","uuid":"3cee28ab-3278-444d-840d-decd83e86fb5"}

newCacheSizeBytes);\n    85→            if (caches.values().isEmpty()) {\n    86→                return;\n    87→            }\n    88→            final CircularIterator<NamedCache> circularIterator = new CircularIterator<>(caches.values());\n    89→            while (sizeInBytes.get() > maxCacheSizeBytes) {\n    90→                final NamedCache cache = circularIterator.next();\n    91→                synchronized (cache) {\n    92→                    final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n    93→                    cache.evict();\n    94→ {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LubCALdDREF8pSK2d9HW2j","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now let me prepare my comprehensive report with all findings:\n\n## Comprehensive Concurrency Bug Analysis Report\n\nI have conducted a thorough search of the Kafka Streams source code for concurrency-related bugs, specifically focusing on deadlock and hang potential. Here are my findings:\n\n### CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND:\n\n#### 1. **POTENTIAL LOST WAKEUP in DefaultStateUpdater.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Lines:** 446-459 (waitIfAllChangelogsCompletelyRead method)\n**Issue:** Race condition with lost wakeup possibility\n```java\nwhile (allWorkDone() && fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId == -1L) {\n    isIdle.set(true);\n    tasksAndActionsCondition.await();  // Line 450\n}\n// ...\nfinally {\n    tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n    isIdle.set(false);  // Line 457\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** The condition `allWorkDone()` is checked without holding the lock initially, then another check happens inside the while loop. If work becomes available between the first check and the await(), it won't wake up because signalAll() may have already been called.\n\n**Additional Issue:** Lines 322-327 - `restoreConsumerInstanceId()` calls `tasksAndActionsCondition.signalAll()` but doesn't check if the thread is actually waiting. This could mask missed signals.\n\n---\n\n#### 2. **NESTED LOCK DEADLOCK RISK in DefaultStateUpdater.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Lines:** 1088-1099 (executeWithQueuesLocked method)\n**Issue:** Multiple locks acquired in fixed order with potential for circular dependency\n```java\nprivate <T> Set<T> executeWithQueuesLocked(final Supplier<Set<T>> action) {\n    tasksAndActionsLock.lock();      // Line 1089\n    restoredActiveTasksLock.lock();  // Line 1090\n    exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.lock(); // Line 1091\n    try {\n        return action.get();\n    } finally {\n        exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.unlock();\n        restoredActiveTasksLock.unlock();\n        tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** Three locks are always acquired in this specific order. If other code paths acquire any subset of these locks in a different order, deadlock is possible. The supplier action could also try to acquire locks.\n\n---\n\n#### 3. **DOUBLE NESTED SYNCHRONIZATION in CachingKeyValueStore.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/CachingKeyValueStore.java`\n\n**Lines:** 118-122 (getPosition method)\n**Issue:** Nested synchronized blocks on related objects\n```java\nsynchronized (position) {\n    synchronized (wrappedPosition) {\n        mergedPosition.merge(position);\n        mergedPosition.merge(wrappedPosition);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** Two Position objects are locked in nested fashion. If another thread locks them in reverse order, deadlock occurs. The `wrappedPosition` is obtained from `wrapped().getPosition()` which could return the same object in some scenarios.\n\n**Additional Lines:** 277-295 (putInternal) - Also uses `synchronized (position)` after acquiring write lock, creating lock ordering issues.\n\n---\n\n#### 4. **READWRITE LOCK WITH NESTED SYNCHRONIZED in CachingKeyValueStore.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/CachingKeyValueStore.java`\n\n**Lines:** 265-272 (put method), 319-328 (putAll method)\n**Issue:** Write lock followed by nested synchronized block\n```java\nlock.writeLock().lock();  // ReadWriteLock\ntry {\n    validateStoreOpen();\n    for (final KeyValue<Bytes, byte[]> entry : entries) {\n        put(entry.key, entry.value);  // Recursive call\n    }\n} finally {\n    lock.writeLock().unlock();\n}\n```\n\n**Problem in putAll:** Calls `put()` which also tries to acquire the write lock - ReentrantReadWriteLock is reentrant, but the nested synchronized calls could cause ordering issues.\n\n---\n\n#### 5. **CONDITION VARIABLE WITH MISSING LOCK CHECKS in DefaultStateUpdater.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Lines:** 322-327 (restoreConsumerInstanceId method)\n**Issue:** Condition signal without verification thread is waiting\n```java\nif (setDeadline) {\n    fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId = time.milliseconds() + timeout.toMillis();\n    tasksAndActionsLock.lock();\n    try {\n        tasksAndActionsCondition.signalAll();  // Line 324 - might signal before await\n    } finally {\n        tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** If `signalAll()` is called before the thread enters `await()` at line 450, the wakeup is lost.\n\n---\n\n#### 6. **THREAD CACHE SYNCHRONIZATION ISSUES in ThreadCache.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/ThreadCache.java`\n\n**Lines:** 80-101 (resize method)\n**Issue:** Method-level synchronized with nested object synchronization\n```java\npublic synchronized void resize(final long newCacheSizeBytes) {\n    // ... \n    synchronized (cache) {  // Line 91\n        final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n        cache.evict();\n        sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** The entire method is synchronized, then individual cache objects are locked. This could deadlock if other threads call synchronized cache operations while a resize is in progress.\n\n---\n\n#### 7. **POTENTIAL DEADLOCK in TopologyMetadata.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/TopologyMetadata.java`\n\n**Lines:** 152-158, 220-227, 263-279\n**Issue:** Lock acquired and released in nested calls\n```java\nprivate void lock() {\n    version.topologyLock.lock();  // Line 153\n}\n\npublic void wakeupThreads() {\n    try {\n        lock();  // Line 222\n        version.topologyCV.signalAll();  // Line 223\n    } finally {\n        unlock();\n    }\n}\n\npublic void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology(final Supplier<StreamThread.State> threadState) {\n    if (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n        try {\n            lock();  // Line 266\n            while (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n                try {\n                    version.topologyCV.await();  // Line 270 - Releases lock\n                } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n                    log.error(\"...\");  // Caught but no re-check of condition\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** After InterruptedException is caught at line 271, the code continues without rechecking the while condition. This can result in exiting the wait loop even though the topology is still empty.\n\n---\n\n#### 8. **MULTIPLE SYNCHRONIZED BLOCKS ACROSS THREAD BOUNDARIES in KafkaStreams.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/KafkaStreams.java`\n\n**Lines:** 1267-1279, 1286-1298\n**Issue:** Synchronized iteration over shared list\n```java\nsynchronized (threads) {\n    processStreamThread(thread -> {\n        if (thread.state() == StreamThread.State.DEAD) {\n            // May call other synchronized methods on threads\n        }\n    });\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** The code comment at lines 1258-1260 explicitly warns about deadlock risk from nested locks. The solution is to copy the list, but not all code paths follow this pattern consistently.\n\n---\n\n#### 9. **ATOMIC BOOLEAN WITH CONDITION VARIABLE RACE in DefaultStateUpdater.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Lines:** 449, 457, 86-87\n**Issue:** AtomicBoolean set outside lock scope\n```java\nprivate final AtomicBoolean isIdle = new AtomicBoolean(false);\n\nprivate void waitIfAllChangelogsCompletelyRead() {\n    tasksAndActionsLock.lock();\n    try {\n        while (allWorkDone() && fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId == -1L) {\n            isIdle.set(true);  // Line 449 - inside lock\n            tasksAndActionsCondition.await();\n        }\n    } finally {\n        tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n        isIdle.set(false);  // Line 457 - OUTSIDE lock!\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** `isIdle.set(false)` is called AFTER releasing the lock. Between the finally block and the set, other threads could read inconsistent state.\n\n---\n\n#### 10. **CONDITION VARIABLE WITHOUT SPURIOUS WAKEUP LOOP in TopologyMetadata.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/TopologyMetadata.java`\n\n**Lines:** 268-274\n**Issue:** Condition variable await with broken loop\n```java\nwhile (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n    try {\n        version.topologyCV.await();\n    } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n        log.error(\"StreamThread was interrupted while waiting on empty topology\", e);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** InterruptedException is caught but not rethrown or converted. Here are my findings:\n\n### CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND:\n\n#### 1. **POTENTIAL LOST WAKEUP in DefaultStateUpdater.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Lines:** 446-459 (waitIfAllChangelogsCompletelyRead method)\n**Issue:** Race condition with lost wakeup possibility\n```java\nwhile (allWorkDone() && fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId == -1L) {\n    isIdle.set(true);\n    tasksAndActionsCondition.await();  // Line 450\n}\n// ...\nfinally {\n    tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n    isIdle.set(false);  // Line 457\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** The condition `allWorkDone()` is checked without holding the lock initially, then another check happens inside the while loop. If work becomes available between the first check and the await(), it won't wake up because signalAll() may have already been called.\n\n**Additional Issue:** Lines 322-327 - `restoreConsumerInstanceId()` calls `tasksAndActionsCondition.signalAll()` but doesn't check if the thread is actually waiting. This could mask missed signals.\n\n---\n\n#### 2. **NESTED LOCK DEADLOCK RISK in DefaultStateUpdater.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Lines:** 1088-1099 (executeWithQueuesLocked method)\n**Issue:** Multiple locks acquired in fixed order with potential for circular dependency\n```java\nprivate <T> Set<T> executeWithQueuesLocked(final Supplier<Set<T>> action) {\n    tasksAndActionsLock.lock();      // Line 1089\n    restoredActiveTasksLock.lock();  // Line 1090\n    exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.lock(); // Line 1091\n    try {\n        return action.get();\n    } finally {\n        exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.unlock();\n        restoredActiveTasksLock.unlock();\n        tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** Three locks are always acquired in this specific order. If other code paths acquire any subset of these locks in a different order, deadlock is possible. The supplier action could also try to acquire locks.\n\n---\n\n#### 3. **DOUBLE NESTED SYNCHRONIZATION in CachingKeyValueStore.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/CachingKeyValueStore.java`\n\n**Lines:** 118-122 (getPosition method)\n**Issue:** Nested synchronized blocks on related objects\n```java\nsynchronized (position) {\n    synchronized (wrappedPosition) {\n        mergedPosition.merge(position);\n        mergedPosition.merge(wrappedPosition);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** Two Position objects are locked in nested fashion. If another thread locks them in reverse order, deadlock occurs. The `wrappedPosition` is obtained from `wrapped().getPosition()` which could return the same object in some scenarios.\n\n**Additional Lines:** 277-295 (putInternal) - Also uses `synchronized (position)` after acquiring write lock, creating lock ordering issues.\n\n---\n\n#### 4. **READWRITE LOCK WITH NESTED SYNCHRONIZED in CachingKeyValueStore.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/CachingKeyValueStore.java`\n\n**Lines:** 265-272 (put method), 319-328 (putAll method)\n**Issue:** Write lock followed by nested synchronized block\n```java\nlock.writeLock().lock(); This could deadlock if other threads call synchronized cache operations while a resize is in progress.\n\n---\n\n#### 7. **POTENTIAL DEADLOCK in TopologyMetadata.java**\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/TopologyMetadata.java`\n\n**Lines:** 152-158, 220-227, 263-279\n**Issue:** Lock acquired and released in nested calls\n```java\nprivate void lock() {\n    version.topologyLock.lock();  // Line 153\n}\n\npublic void wakeupThreads() {\n    try {\n        lock();  // Line 222\n        version.topologyCV.signalAll();  // Line 223\n    } finally {\n        unlock();\n    }\n}\n\npublic void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology(final Supplier<StreamThread.State> threadState) {\n    if (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n        try {\n            lock();  // Line 266\n            while (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n                try {\n                    version.topologyCV.await();  // Line 270 - Releases lock\n                } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n                    log.error(\"...\");  // Caught but no re-check of condition\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:** After InterruptedException is caught at line 271, the code continues without rechecking the while condition.    } else {\n   156→                    result = (QueryResult<R>) handler.apply(\n   157→                        query,\n   158→                        mergedPosition,\n   159→                        positionBound,\n   160→                        config,\n   161→                        this\n   162→                    );\n   163→                }\n   164→            } finally {\n   165→                lock.unlock();\n   166→            }\n   167→        }\n   168→        if (config.isCollectExecutionInfo()) {\n   169→            result.addExecutionInfo(\n   170→                \"Handled in \" + getClass() + \" in \" + (System.nanoTime() - start) + \"ns\");\n   171→        }\n   172→        return result;\n   173→    }\n   174→\n   175→    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   176→    private <R> QueryResult<R> runKeyQuery(final Query<R> query,\n   177→                                           final Position mergedPosition,\n   178→                                           final PositionBound positionBound,\n   179→                                           final QueryConfig config) {\n   180→        if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE_WITH_HEADERS) {\n   181→            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Queries (IQv2) are not supported for timestamped key-value stores with headers yet.\");\n   182→        }\n   183→        QueryResult<R> result = null;\n   184→        final KeyQuery<Bytes, byte[]> keyQuery = (KeyQuery<Bytes, byte[]>) query;\n   185→\n   186→        if (keyQuery.isSkipCache()) {\n   187→            return wrapped().query(query, positionBound, config);\n   188→        }\n   189→\n   190→        final Bytes key = keyQuery.getKey();\n   191→\n   192→        synchronized (mergedPosition) {\n   193→            if (internalContext.cache() != null) {\n   194→                final LRUCacheEntry lruCacheEntry = internalContext.cache().get(cacheName, key);\n   195→                if (lruCacheEntry != null) {\n   196→                    final byte[] rawValue;\n   197→                    if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE && !WrappedStateStore.isTimestamped(wrapped()) && !StoreQueryUtils.isAdapter(wrapped())) {\n   198→                        rawValue = ValueAndTimestampDeserializer.rawValue(lruCacheEntry.value());\n   199→                    } else {\n   200→                        rawValue = lruCacheEntry.value();\n   201→                    }\n   202→                    result = (QueryResult<R>) QueryResult.forResult(rawValue);\n   203→                }\n   204→            }\n   205→\n   206→            // We don't need to check the position at the state store since we already performed the check on\n   207→            // the merged position above\n   208→            if (result == null) {\n   209→                result = wrapped().query(query, PositionBound.unbounded(), config);\n   210→            }\n   211→            result.setPosition(mergedPosition.copy());\n   212→        }\n   213→        return result;\n   214→    }\n   215→\n   216→    private void putAndMaybeForward(final ThreadCache.DirtyEntry entry,\n   217→                                    final InternalProcessorContext<?, ?> context) {\n   218→        if (flushListener != null) {\n   219→            final byte[] rawNewValue = entry.newValue();\n   220→            final byte[] rawOldValue = rawNewValue == null || sendOldValues ? wrapped().get(entry.key()) : null;\n   221→\n   222→            // this is an optimization: if this key did not exist in underlying store and also not in the cache,\n   223→            // we can skip flushing to downstream as well as writing to underlying store\n   224→            if (rawNewValue != null || rawOldValue != null) {\n   225→                // we need to get the old values if needed, and then put to store, and then flush\n   226→                final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n   227→                try {\n   228→                    context.setRecordContext(entry.entry().context());\n   229→                    wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.newValue());\n   230→                    flushListener.apply(\n   231→                        new Record<>(\n   232→                            entry.key().get(),\n   233→                            new Change<>(rawNewValue, sendOldValues ? rawOldValue : null),\n   234→                            entry.entry().context().timestamp(),\n   235→                            entry.entry().context().headers()));\n   236→                } finally {\n   237→                    context.setRecordContext(current);\n   238→                }\n   239→            }\n   240→        } else {\n   241→            final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n   242→            try {\n   243→                context.setRecordContext(entry.entry().context());\n   244→                wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.newValue());\n   245→            } finally {\n   246→                context.setRecordContext(current);\n   247→            }\n   248→        }\n   249→    }\n   250→\n   251→    @Override\n   252→    public boolean setFlushListener(final CacheFlushListener<byte[], byte[]> flushListener,\n   253→                                    final boolean sendOldValues) {\n   254→        this.flushListener = flushListener;\n   255→        this.sendOldValues = sendOldValues;\n   256→\n   257→        return true;\n   258→    }\n   259→\n   260→    @Override\n   261→    public void put(final Bytes key,\n   262→                    final byte[] value) {\n   263→        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n   264→        validateStoreOpen();\n   265→        lock.writeLock().lock();\n   266→        try {\n   267→            validateStoreOpen();\n   268→            // for null bytes, we still put it into cache indicating tombstones\n   269→            putInternal(key, value);\n   270→        } finally {\n   271→            lock.writeLock().unlock();\n   272→        }\n   273→    }\n   274→\n   275→    private void putInternal(final Bytes key,\n   276→                             final byte[] value) {\n   277→        synchronized (position) {\n   278→            internalContext.cache().put(\n   279→                cacheName,\n   280→                key,\n   281→                new LRUCacheEntry(\n   282→                    value,\n   283→                    internalContext.recordContext().headers(),\n   284→                    true,\n   285→                    internalContext.recordContext().offset(),\n   286→                    internalContext.recordContext().timestamp(),\n   287→                    internalContext.recordContext().partition(),\n   288→                    internalContext.recordContext().topic(),\n   289→                    internalContext.recordContext().sourceRawKey(),\n   290→                    internalContext.recordContext().sourceRawValue()\n   291→                )\n   292→            );\n   293→\n   294→            StoreQueryUtils.updatePosition(position, internalContext);\n   295→        }\n   296→    }\n   297→\n   298→    @Override\n   299→    public byte[] putIfAbsent(final Bytes key,\n   300→                              final byte[] value) {\n   301→        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n   302→        validateStoreOpen();\n   303→        lock.writeLock().lock();\n   304→        try {\n   305→            validateStoreOpen();\n   306→            final byte[] v = getInternal(key);\n   307→            if (v == null) {\n   308→                putInternal(key, value);\n   309→            }\n   310→            return v;\n   311→        } finally {\n   312→            lock.writeLock().unlock();\n   313→        }\n   314→    }\n   315→\n   316→    @Override\n   317→    public void putAll(final List<KeyValue<Bytes, byte[]>> entries) {\n   318→        validateStoreOpen();\n   319→        lock.writeLock().lock();\n   320→        try {\n   321→            validateStoreOpen();\n   322→            for (final KeyValue<Bytes, byte[]> entry : entries) {\n   323→                Objects.requireNonNull(entry.key, \"key cannot be null\");\n   324→                put(entry.key, entry.value);\n   325→            }\n   326→        } finally {\n   327→            lock.writeLock().unlock();\n   328→        }\n   329→    }\n   330→\n   331→    @Override\n   332→    public byte[] delete(final Bytes key) {\n   333→        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n   334→        validateStoreOpen();\n   335→ putInternal(key, null);\n   347→        return v;\n   348→    }\n   349→\n   350→    @Override\n   351→    public byte[] get(final Bytes key) {\n   352→        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n   353→        validateStoreOpen();\n   354→        final Lock theLock;\n   355→        if (Thread.currentThread().equals(streamThread)) {\n   356→            theLock = lock.writeLock();\n   357→        } else {\n   358→            theLock = lock.readLock();\n   359→        }\n   360→        theLock.lock();\n   361→        try {\n   362→            validateStoreOpen();\n   363→            return getInternal(key);\n   364→        } finally {\n   365→            theLock.unlock();\n   366→        }\n   367→    }\n   368→\n   369→    private byte[] getInternal(final Bytes key) {\n   370→        LRUCacheEntry entry = null;\n   371→        if (internalContext.cache() != null) {\n   372→            entry = internalContext.cache().get(cacheName, key);\n   373→        }\n   374→        if (entry == null) {\n   375→            final byte[] rawValue = wrapped().get(key);\n   376→            if (rawValue == null) {\n   377→                return null;\n   378→            }\n   379→            // only update the cache if this call is on the streamThread\n   380→            // as we don't want other threads to trigger an eviction/flush\n   381→            if (Thread.currentThread().equals(streamThread)) {\n   382→                internalContext.cache().put(cacheName, key, new LRUCacheEntry(rawValue));\n   383→            }\n   384→ 394→            LOG.warn(\"Returning empty iterator for fetch with invalid key range: from > to. \" +\n   395→                \"This may be due to range arguments set in the wrong order, \" +\n   396→ internalContext.cache().flush(cacheName);\n   469→            wrapped().commit(changelogOffsets);\n   470→        } finally {\n   471→            lock.writeLock().unlock();\n   472→        }\n   473→    }\n   474→\n   475→    @Override\n   476→    public void flushCache() {\n   477→        validateStoreOpen();\n   478→        lock.writeLock().lock();\n   479→        try {\n   480→   499→    @Override\n   500→    public void close() {\n   501→        lock.writeLock().lock();\n   502→        try {\n   503→            final LinkedList<RuntimeException> suppressed = executeAll(\n   504→                () -> internalContext.cache().flush(cacheName),\n   505→                () -> internalContext.cache().close(cacheName),\n   506→                wrapped()::close\n   507→            );\n   508→            if (!suppressed.isEmpty()) {\n   509→                throwSuppressed(\"Caught an exception while closing caching key value store for store \" + name(),\n   510→                    suppressed);\n   511→            }\n   512→        } finally {\n   513→            lock.writeLock().unlock();\n   514→        }\n   515→    }\n   516→\n   517→    public enum CacheType {\n   518→        KEY_VALUE_STORE,\n   519→        TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE,\n   520→        TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE_WITH_HEADERS\n   521→    }\n   522→}\n org.apache.kafka.streams.state.internals.ExceptionUtils.executeAll;\nimport static org.apache.kafka.streams.state.internals.ExceptionUtils.throwSuppressed;\n\npublic class CachingKeyValueStore\n    extends WrappedStateStore<KeyValueStore<Bytes, byte[]>, byte[], byte[]>\n    implements KeyValueStore<Bytes, byte[]>, CachedStateStore<byte[], byte[]> {\n\n    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CachingKeyValueStore.class);\n\n    private CacheFlushListener<byte[], byte[]> flushListener;\n    private boolean sendOldValues;\n    private String cacheName;\n    private InternalProcessorContext<?, ?> internalContext;\n    private Thread streamThread;\n    private final ReadWriteLock lock = new ReentrantReadWriteLock();\n    private final Position position;\n    private final CacheType cacheType;\n\n    @FunctionalInterface\n    public interface CacheQueryHandler {\n        QueryResult<?> apply(\n            final Query<?> query,\n            final Position mergedPosition,\n            final PositionBound positionBound,\n            final QueryConfig config,\n            final StateStore store\n        );\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"rawtypes\")\n    private final Map<Class, CacheQueryHandler> queryHandlers =\n        mkMap(\n            mkEntry(\n                KeyQuery.class,\n                (query, mergedPosition, positionBound, config, store) ->\n                    runKeyQuery(query, mergedPosition, positionBound, config)\n            )\n        );\n\n\n    CachingKeyValueStore(final KeyValueStore<Bytes, byte[]> underlying, final CacheType cacheType) {\n        super(underlying);\n        position = Position.emptyPosition();\n        this.cacheType = cacheType;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void init(final StateStoreContext stateStoreContext, final StateStore root) {\n        internalContext = asInternalProcessorContext(stateStoreContext);\n        cacheName = ThreadCache.nameSpaceFromTaskIdAndStore(internalContext.taskId().toString(), name());\n        internalContext.registerCacheFlushListener(cacheName, entries -> {\n            for (final ThreadCache.DirtyEntry entry : entries) {\n                putAndMaybeForward(entry, internalContext);\n            }\n        });\n        super.init(stateStoreContext, root);\n        // save the stream thread as we only ever want to trigger a flush\n        // when the stream thread is the current thread.\n        streamThread = Thread.currentThread();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Position getPosition() {\n        // We return the merged position since the query uses the merged position as well\n        final Position mergedPosition = Position.emptyPosition();\n        final Position wrappedPosition = wrapped().getPosition();\n        synchronized (position) {\n            synchronized (wrappedPosition) {\n                mergedPosition.merge(position);\n                mergedPosition.merge(wrappedPosition);\n            }\n        }\n        return mergedPosition;\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    @Override\n    public <R> QueryResult<R> query(final Query<R> query,\n                                    final PositionBound positionBound,\n                                    final QueryConfig config) {\n\n        if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE_WITH_HEADERS) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Queries (IQv2) are not supported for timestamped key-value stores with headers yet.\");\n        }\n\n        final long start = config.isCollectExecutionInfo() ? System.nanoTime() : -1L;\n        final QueryResult<R> result;\n\n        final CacheQueryHandler handler = queryHandlers.get(query.getClass());\n        if (handler == null) {\n            result = wrapped().query(query, positionBound, config);\n\n        } else {\n            final int partition = internalContext.taskId().partition();\n            final Lock lock = this.lock.readLock();\n            lock.lock();\n            try {\n                validateStoreOpen();\n                final Position mergedPosition = getPosition();\n\n                // We use the merged position since the cache and the store may be at different positions\n                if (!StoreQueryUtils.isPermitted(mergedPosition, positionBound, partition)) {\n                    result = QueryResult.notUpToBound(mergedPosition, positionBound, partition);\n                } else {\n                    result = (QueryResult<R>) handler.apply(\n                        query,\n                        mergedPosition,\n                        positionBound,\n                        config,\n                        this\n                    );\n                }\n            } finally {\n                lock.unlock();\n            }\n        }\n        if (config.isCollectExecutionInfo()) {\n            result.addExecutionInfo(\n                \"Handled in \" + getClass() + \" in \" + (System.nanoTime() - start) + \"ns\");\n        }\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private <R> QueryResult<R> runKeyQuery(final Query<R> query,\n                                           final Position mergedPosition,\n                                           final PositionBound positionBound,\n                                           final QueryConfig config) {\n        if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE_WITH_HEADERS) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Queries (IQv2) are not supported for timestamped key-value stores with headers yet.\");\n        }\n        QueryResult<R> result = null;\n        final KeyQuery<Bytes, byte[]> keyQuery = (KeyQuery<Bytes, byte[]>) query;\n\n        if (keyQuery.isSkipCache()) {\n            return wrapped().query(query, positionBound, config);\n        }\n\n        final Bytes key = keyQuery.getKey();\n\n        synchronized (mergedPosition) {\n            if (internalContext.cache() != null) {\n                final LRUCacheEntry lruCacheEntry = internalContext.cache().get(cacheName, key);\n                if (lruCacheEntry != null) {\n                    final byte[] rawValue;\n                    if (cacheType == CacheType.TIMESTAMPED_KEY_VALUE_STORE && !WrappedStateStore.isTimestamped(wrapped()) && !StoreQueryUtils.isAdapter(wrapped())) {\n                        rawValue = ValueAndTimestampDeserializer.rawValue(lruCacheEntry.value());\n                    } else {\n                        rawValue = lruCacheEntry.value();\n                    }\n                    result = (QueryResult<R>) QueryResult.forResult(rawValue);\n                }\n            }\n\n            // We don't need to check the position at the state store since we already performed the check on\n            // the merged position above\n            if (result == null) {\n                result = wrapped().query(query, PositionBound.unbounded(), config);\n            }\n            result.setPosition(mergedPosition.copy());\n        }\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    private void putAndMaybeForward(final ThreadCache.DirtyEntry entry,\n                                    final InternalProcessorContext<?, ?> context) {\n        if (flushListener != null) {\n            final byte[] rawNewValue = entry.newValue();\n            final byte[] rawOldValue = rawNewValue == null || sendOldValues ? wrapped().get(entry.key()) : null;\n\n            // this is an optimization: if this key did not exist in underlying store and also not in the cache,\n            // we can skip flushing to downstream as well as writing to underlying store\n            if (rawNewValue != null || rawOldValue != null) {\n                // we need to get the old values if needed, and then put to store, and then flush\n                final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n                try {\n                    context.setRecordContext(entry.entry().context());\n                    wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.newValue());\n                    flushListener.apply(\n                        new Record<>(\n                            entry.key().get(),\n                            new Change<>(rawNewValue, sendOldValues ? rawOldValue : null),\n                            entry.entry().context().timestamp(),\n                            entry.entry().context().headers()));\n                } finally {\n                    context.setRecordContext(current);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            final ProcessorRecordContext current = context.recordContext();\n            try {\n                context.setRecordContext(entry.entry().context());\n                wrapped().put(entry.key(), entry.newValue());\n            } finally {\n                context.setRecordContext(current);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean setFlushListener(final CacheFlushListener<byte[], byte[]> flushListener,\n                                    final boolean sendOldValues) {\n        this.flushListener = flushListener;\n        this.sendOldValues = sendOldValues;\n\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void put(final Bytes key,\n                    final byte[] value) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n        validateStoreOpen();\n        lock.writeLock().lock();\n        try {\n            validateStoreOpen();\n            // for null bytes, we still put it into cache indicating tombstones\n            putInternal(key, value);\n        } finally {\n            lock.writeLock().unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void putInternal(final Bytes key,\n                             final byte[] value) {\n        synchronized (position) {\n            internalContext.cache().put(\n                cacheName,\n                key,\n                new LRUCacheEntry(\n                    value,\n                    internalContext.recordContext().headers(),\n                    true,\n                    internalContext.recordContext().offset(),\n                    internalContext.recordContext().timestamp(),\n                    internalContext.recordContext().partition(),\n                    internalContext.recordContext().topic(),\n                    internalContext.recordContext().sourceRawKey(),\n                    internalContext.recordContext().sourceRawValue()\n                )\n            );\n\n            StoreQueryUtils.updatePosition(position, internalContext);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public byte[] putIfAbsent(final Bytes key,\n                              final byte[] value) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key cannot be null\");\n        validateStoreOpen();\n        lock.writeLock().lock();\n        try {\n            validateStoreOpen();\n            final byte[] v = getInternal(key);\n            if (v == null) {\n                putInternal(key, value);\n            }\n            return v;\n        } finally {\n     put(entry.key, entry.value);\n            }\n        } finally {\n            lock.writeLock().unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public byte[] delete(final Bytes key) {\n internalContext.cache().get(cacheName, key);\n        }\n        if (entry == null) {\n            final byte[] rawValue = wrapped().get(key);\n            if (rawValue == null) {\n
